We like your project, but two hundred thousand is too high. Can you give us a discount?
I understand. If you sign this month, we will give you a five percent discount.
Five percent is not enough. Our counter-offer is one hundred and seventy thousand.
That is below our cost. But if you pay the whole amount in advance, we will accept one hundred and eighty.
We prefer to pay in three installments. Are you flexible on that?
We are flexible. If you pay a deposit today, we will accept three installments. Then we have a deal.

Good morning. Thank you for coming to discuss our offer. Let me be clear about the numbers. Our first quote is two hundred thousand reais for the full project. If you sign this month, we will give you a five percent discount. If you pay the whole amount in advance, we will offer an extra two percent. We are flexible on the timeline, but the price is already very competitive. Of course, if you prefer to pay in installments, we will accept three equal payments, but in that case the discount will be smaller. Please look at the quote, talk to your team, and send us your counter-offer. If your numbers are reasonable, we will close the deal quickly.

After thirty years of business, I have learned one thing about negotiation. A good deal is not when one side wins and the other side loses. A good deal is when both sides leave the table happy. So never accept the first offer, but never be greedy either. Always prepare your counter-offer before the meeting. Know your maximum price and your minimum price. If the other side gives you a discount, give them something in return, like a faster payment or a longer contract. Listen more than you speak. And remember: if you stay calm and respectful, you will get a better deal than if you fight. The best negotiators are not the loudest. They are the most patient.

First, the seller sends a quote with the first price. Then, the buyer studies the offer and prepares a counter-offer. Next, both sides meet at the table and discuss the terms. After that, they bargain: if one side gives a discount, the other side offers something in return. Finally, when both sides agree, they shake hands and close the deal.
